steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that Venus has whistler waves with energy signatures like Earth's (which come from lightning), yet observations of other lightning-consistent phenomena — like light flashes — are rare. The question asks which choice best explains this mismatch.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Venus has distinct geophysical characteristics that promote whistler wave generation through a non-lightning mechanism': explains why whistler waves exist without visible lightning phenomena. ✓
  • Choice B — 'the energy signatures are not actually similar to Earth's': contradicts the passage, which states they are similar. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Earth's lightning does not produce whistler waves': contradicts established science the passage relies on. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the spacecraft's instruments had measurement errors': invokes error rather than explaining the underlying phenomenon. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
